revision.comment: mistake
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=龍田神社| 所在地=奈良県生駒郡斑鳩町龍田1-5-6| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|30|N|135|43|30|E|}}| 祭神=天御柱命
國御柱命
竜田比古神
竜田比女神| 社格=式内社（小）・県社| 創建=崇神天皇の御代| 例祭=[[10月15日]]に近い日曜日| 本殿=[[流造]]| 画像=[[画像:Tatsuta-Jinja02.JPG|250px]]
龍田神社拝殿| }} '''龍田神社'''（たつたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[斑鳩町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==歴史== [[崇神天皇]]の時代に創立され、[[法隆寺]]の鎮守とされていた。伝承によれば、[[聖徳太子]]が[[法隆寺]]の建設地を探し求めていたときに、白髪の老人に化身した龍田大明神に逢い、「斑鳩の里こそが仏法興隆の地である。私はその守護神となろう」と言われたので、その地に法隆寺を建立し、鎮守社として龍田大明神を祀る神社を創建したという。元々の社名は「龍田比古龍田比女神社」で、その名の通り龍田比古神・龍田比女神の二神（龍田大明神）を祀っていた。[[延喜式神名帳]]にもこの名前で記載され、小社に列している。しかし、後に[[龍田大社]]より天御柱命・國御柱命の二神を勧請したため、元々の祭神は忘れられてしまった。現在は天御柱命・國御柱命を主祭神とし、龍田比古神・龍田比女神を配祀している。 明治の[[神仏分離]]により法隆寺から離れ、三郷町立野の龍田大社の[[摂社]]となった。独立の請願の結果、大正11年3月に龍田大社より独立し、県社に列格した。 {{Commons|Category:Tatsuta-Jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たつたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:府県社]] {{shinto-stub}}

竜田比古神
竜田比女神| 社格=式内社（小）・県社| 創建=崇神天皇の御代| 例祭=[[10月15日]]に近い日曜日| 本殿=[[流造]]| 画像=[[ファイル:Tatsuta-jinja Ikaruga Nara Pref01s3s4410.jpg|300px]]| }} [[ファイル:Tatsuta-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|拝殿]] '''龍田神社'''（たつたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[斑鳩町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==歴史== [[崇神天皇]]の時代に創立され、[[法隆寺]]の鎮守とされていた。伝承によれば、[[聖徳太子]]が[[法隆寺]]の建設地を探し求めていたときに、白髪の老人に化身した龍田大明神に逢い、「斑鳩の里こそが仏法興隆の地である。私はその守護神となろう」と言われたので、その地に法隆寺を建立し、鎮守社として龍田大明神を祀る神社を創建したという。元々の社名は「龍田比古龍田比女神社」で、その名の通り龍田比古神・龍田比女神の二神（龍田大明神）を祀っていた。[[延喜式神名帳]]にもこの名前で記載され、小社に列している。しかし、後に[[龍田大社]]より天御柱命・國御柱命の二神を勧請したため、元々の祭神は忘れられてしまった。現在は天御柱命・國御柱命を主祭神とし、龍田比古神・龍田比女神を配祀している。 明治の[[神仏分離]]により法隆寺から離れ、三郷町立野の龍田大社の[[摂社]]となった。独立の請願の結果、大正11年3月に龍田大社より独立し、県社に列格した。 == 交通アクセス == *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[大和路線]]・[[近鉄生駒線]][[王寺駅]]より[[奈良交通]]バスで8分、「[[龍田神社]]前」下車、徒歩すぐ == 周辺 == *[[吉田寺]] *[[竜田川]]、[[奈良県立竜田公園]] *[[藤ノ木古墳]] *[[法隆寺]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www4.kcn.ne.jp/~ikaru-i/spot2/tatutazinnzya.html 龍田神社-法隆寺iセンター・斑鳩町観光協会]{{Commons|Category:Tatsuta-Jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たつたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:府県社]] {{shinto-stub}}

竜田比女神| 社格=式内社（小）・県社| 創建=崇神天皇の御代| 例祭=[[10月15日]]に近い日曜日| 本殿=[[流造]]| 画像=[[ファイル:Tatsuta-jinja Ikaruga Nara Pref01s3s4410.jpg|300px]]| }} [[ファイル:Tatsuta-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|拝殿]] '''龍田神社'''（たつたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[斑鳩町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==歴史== [[崇神天皇]]の時代に創立され、[[法隆寺]]の鎮守とされていた。伝承によれば、[[聖徳太子]]が[[法隆寺]]の建設地を探し求めていたときに、白髪の老人に化身した龍田大明神に逢い、「斑鳩の里こそが仏法興隆の地である。私はその守護神となろう」と言われたので、その地に法隆寺を建立し、鎮守社として龍田大明神を祀る神社を創建したという。元々の社名は「龍田比古龍田比女神社」で、その名の通り龍田比古神・龍田比女神の二神（龍田大明神）を祀っていた。[[延喜式神名帳]]にもこの名前で記載され、小社に列している。しかし、後に[[龍田大社]]より天御柱命・國御柱命の二神を勧請したため、元々の祭神は忘れられてしまった。現在は天御柱命・國御柱命を主祭神とし、龍田比古神・龍田比女神を配祀している。 明治の[[神仏分離]]により法隆寺から離れ、三郷町立野の[[官幣大社]]龍田神社（現・[[龍田大社]]）の[[摂社]]となった。独立の請願の結果、大正11年3月に龍田大社より独立し、県社に列格した。 == 交通アクセス == *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[大和路線]]・[[近鉄生駒線]][[王寺駅]]より[[奈良交通]]バスで8分、「[[龍田神社]]前」下車、徒歩すぐ == 周辺 == *[[吉田寺]] *[[竜田川]]、[[奈良県立竜田公園]] *[[藤ノ木古墳]] *[[法隆寺]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www4.kcn.ne.jp/~ikaru-i/spot2/tatutazinnzya.html 龍田神社-法隆寺iセンター・斑鳩町観光協会]{{Commons|Category:Tatsuta-Jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たつたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:斑鳩町]] {{shinto-stub}}

竜田比女神| 社格=式内社（小）・県社| 創建=崇神天皇の御代| 例祭=[[10月15日]]に近い日曜日| 本殿=[[流造]]| 画像=[[ファイル:Tatsuta-jinja Ikaruga Nara Pref01s3s4410.jpg|300px]]| }} [[ファイル:Tatsuta-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|拝殿]] '''龍田神社'''（たつたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[斑鳩町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==歴史== [[崇神天皇]]の時代に創立され、[[法隆寺]]の鎮守とされていた。伝承によれば、[[聖徳太子]]が[[法隆寺]]の建設地を探し求めていたときに、白髪の老人に化身した龍田大明神に逢い、「斑鳩の里こそが仏法興隆の地である。私はその守護神となろう」と言われたので、その地に法隆寺を建立し、鎮守社として龍田大明神を祀る神社を創建したという。元々の社名は「龍田比古龍田比女神社」で、その名の通り龍田比古神・龍田比女神の二神（龍田大明神）を祀っていた。[[延喜式神名帳]]にもこの名前で記載され、小社に列している。しかし、後に[[龍田大社]]より天御柱命・國御柱命の二神を勧請したため、元々の祭神は忘れられてしまった。現在は天御柱命・國御柱命を主祭神とし、龍田比古神・龍田比女神を配祀している。 明治の[[神仏分離]]により法隆寺から離れ、三郷町立野の[[官幣大社]]龍田神社（現・[[龍田大社]]）の[[摂社]]となった。独立の請願の結果、大正11年3月に龍田大社より独立し、県社に列格した。 == 交通アクセス == *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[大和路線]]・[[近鉄生駒線]][[王寺駅]]より[[奈良交通]]バスで8分、「[[龍田神社]]前」下車、徒歩すぐ == 周辺 == *[[吉田寺]] *[[竜田川]]、[[奈良県立竜田公園]] *[[藤ノ木古墳]] *[[法隆寺]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www4.kcn.ne.jp/~ikaru-i/spot2/tatutazinnzya.html 龍田神社-法隆寺iセンター・斑鳩町観光協会]{{Commons|Category:Tatsuta-Jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たつたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:斑鳩町]] [[Category:斑鳩町の歴史]]

竜田比女神 |社格=式内社（小）・県社 |創建=崇神天皇の御代 |例祭=[[10月15日]]に近い日曜日 |本殿=[[流造]] |画像=[[ファイル:Tatsuta-jinja Ikaruga Nara Pref01s3s4410.jpg|300px]] }} [[ファイル:Tatsuta-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|拝殿]] '''龍田神社'''（たつたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[斑鳩町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==歴史== [[崇神天皇]]の時代に創立され、[[法隆寺]]の鎮守とされていた。伝承によれば、[[聖徳太子]]が[[法隆寺]]の建設地を探し求めていたときに、白髪の老人に化身した龍田大明神に逢い、「斑鳩の里こそが仏法興隆の地である。私はその守護神となろう」と言われたので、その地に法隆寺を建立し、鎮守社として龍田大明神を祀る神社を創建したという。元々の社名は「龍田比古龍田比女神社」で、その名の通り龍田比古神・龍田比女神の二神（龍田大明神）を祀っていた。[[延喜式神名帳]]にもこの名前で記載され、小社に列している。しかし、後に[[龍田大社]]より[[天御柱命]]・[[国御柱命|國御柱命]]の二神を勧請したため、元々の祭神は忘れられてしまった。現在は天御柱命・國御柱命を主祭神とし、龍田比古神・龍田比女神を配祀している。 明治の[[神仏分離]]により法隆寺から離れ、三郷町立野の[[官幣大社]]龍田神社（現・[[龍田大社]]）の[[摂社]]となった。独立の請願の結果、大正11年3月に龍田大社より独立し、県社に列格した。 かつて[[経蔵]]、[[三重塔]]等が存在したが現在では失われている。 == 交通アクセス == *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[大和路線]]・[[近鉄生駒線]][[王寺駅]]より[[奈良交通]]バスで8分、「龍田神社前」下車、徒歩すぐ == 周辺 == *[[吉田寺]] *[[竜田川]]、[[奈良県立竜田公園]] *[[藤ノ木古墳]] *[[法隆寺]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www4.kcn.ne.jp/~ikaru-i/spot2/tatutazinnzya.html 龍田神社-法隆寺iセンター・斑鳩町観光協会]{{Commons|Category:Tatsuta-Jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たつたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:斑鳩町の建築物]] [[Category:斑鳩町の歴史]] {{shinto-stub}}

國御柱命

旧[[県社]] |創建=[[崇神天皇]]の御代 |例祭=[[10月15日]]に近い日曜日 |本殿=[[流造]] |画像=[[ファイル:Tatsuta-jinja Ikaruga Nara Pref01s3s4410.jpg|300px]] }} [[ファイル:Tatsuta-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|拝殿]] '''龍田神社'''（たつたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[斑鳩町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社|式内小社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==歴史== [[崇神天皇]]の時代に創立され、[[法隆寺]]の鎮守とされていた。伝承によれば、[[聖徳太子]]が[[法隆寺]]の建設地を探し求めていたときに、白髪の老人に化身した龍田大明神に逢い、「斑鳩の里こそが仏法興隆の地である。私はその守護神となろう」と言われたので、その地に法隆寺を建立し、鎮守社として龍田大明神を祀る神社を創建したという。元々の社名は「龍田比古龍田比女神社」で、その名の通り龍田比古神・龍田比女神の二神（龍田大明神）を祀っていた。[[延喜式神名帳]]にもこの名前で記載され、小社に列している。しかし、後に[[龍田大社]]より[[天御柱命]]・[[国御柱命|國御柱命]]の二神を勧請したため、元々の祭神は忘れられてしまった。現在は天御柱命・國御柱命を主祭神とし、龍田比古神・龍田比女神を配祀している。 明治の[[神仏分離]]により法隆寺から離れ、三郷町立野の[[官幣大社]]龍田神社（現・[[龍田大社]]）の[[摂社]]となった。独立の請願の結果、大正11年3月に龍田大社より独立し、県社に列格した。 かつて[[経蔵]]、[[三重塔]]等が存在したが現在では失われている。 == 交通アクセス == *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[大和路線]]・[[近鉄生駒線]][[王寺駅]]より[[奈良交通]]バスで8分、「龍田神社前」下車、徒歩すぐ == 周辺 == *[[吉田寺]] *[[竜田川]]、[[奈良県立竜田公園]] *[[藤ノ木古墳]] *[[法隆寺]] == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Tatsuta-Jinja}} *[http://www4.kcn.ne.jp/~ikaru-i/spot2/tatutazinnzya.html 龍田神社-法隆寺iセンター・斑鳩町観光協会] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たつたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:斑鳩町の建築物]] [[Category:斑鳩町の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{otheruses|奈良県生駒郡斑鳩町にある神社|奈良県生駒郡三郷町にある神社|龍田大社}} {{神社 |名称=龍田神社 |所在地=[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[斑鳩町]]龍田1-5-6 |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 36|緯度秒 = 30 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 30 |祭神=[[天御柱命|天御柱大神]]
[[国御柱命|国御柱大神]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[県社]] |創建= |例祭=[[10月15日]]に近い日曜日 |本殿=[[流造]] |画像=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''龍田神社'''（たつたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[斑鳩町]]龍田にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == 現在の祭神は次の通り境内説明板。。 ; 本殿 :* '''[[天御柱命|天御柱大神]]''' :* '''[[国御柱命|国御柱大神]]''' ; 右社殿 :* 龍田比古大神 :* 龍田比女大神 ; 左社殿 :* [[アメノコヤネ|天児屋根大神]] :* 外三大神 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]における祭神の記載は2座。同帳では「龍田比古龍田比女神社二座」と記載され、元々の祭神は龍田比古神・龍田比女神の2柱であることが知られる。これに関して、[[龍田大社]]から天御柱命・国御柱命の2神が勧請され、元々の祭神が忘れられたとする説がある{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。『[[大和志]]』では、[[龍田大社]]（[[三郷町]]立野）の本宮に対して当社を「龍田新宮」とする{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 == 歴史 == 社伝では、[[聖徳太子]]が[[法隆寺]]の寺地を探し求めていた際、白髪の老人に化身した龍田大明神に逢い、「斑鳩の里こそが仏法興隆の地である。私はその守護神となろう」と言われたので、その地に法隆寺を建立し、鎮守社として龍田大明神を祀る神社を創建したという。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[大和国]][[平群郡]]に「龍田比古龍田比女神社二座」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列している{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 [[明治維新]]後、[[神仏分離]]によって法隆寺から分離し、境内にあった寺院関連建物は全て破却された{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）には三郷町立野の龍田神社（現在の[[龍田大社]]）の[[摂社]]となった{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。[[大正]]11年（[[1922年]]）3月に龍田大社から独立し、[[近代社格制度]]において[[県社]]に列している{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 == 境内 == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|境内社 File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|ソテツ 西株（奈良県指定天然記念物） File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|ソテツ 東株（奈良県指定天然記念物） File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|クス File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|[[金剛流]]発祥之地碑 == 文化財 == === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 天然記念物 ** ソテツの巨樹 - 昭和32年（1957年）6月13日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf 奈良県指定文化財一覧]}}（奈良県ホームページ）。。 === 関連文化財 === * [[法界寺]]本堂（薬師堂） - 国の重要文化財（建造物）。龍田神社神宮寺の伝燈寺（現在は廃寺）から法界寺（[[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]]）へ移築されたもの。明治42年（1909年）4月5日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|1917|法界寺本堂（薬師堂）}}。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[斑鳩町]]龍田1-5-6 '''交通アクセス''' * [[西日本旅客鉄道]]（JR西日本）[[大和路線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道]]（近鉄）[[王寺駅]]から、[[奈良交通]]バスで「龍田神社前」下車（乗車時間約8分、下車後徒歩すぐ） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=龍田神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[龍田大社]] * [[法隆寺]] == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tatsuta-Jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たつたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:斑鳩町の建築物]] [[Category:斑鳩町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]]

旧[[県社]] |創建= |例祭=[[10月15日]]に近い日曜日 |本殿=[[流造]] |画像=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''龍田神社'''（たつたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[斑鳩町]]龍田にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == 現在の祭神は次の通り境内説明板。。 ; 本殿 :* '''[[天御柱命|天御柱大神]]''' :* '''[[国御柱命|国御柱大神]]''' ; 右社殿 :* 龍田比古大神 :* 龍田比女大神 ; 左社殿 :* [[アメノコヤネ|天児屋根大神]] :* 外三大神 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]における祭神の記載は2座。同帳では「龍田比古龍田比女神社二座」と記載され、元々の祭神は龍田比古神・龍田比女神の2柱であることが知られる。これに関して、[[龍田大社]]から天御柱命・国御柱命の2神が勧請され、元々の祭神が忘れられたとする説がある{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。『[[大和志]]』では、[[龍田大社]]（[[三郷町]]立野）の本宮に対して当社を「龍田新宮」とする{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 == 歴史 == 社伝では、[[聖徳太子]]が[[法隆寺]]の寺地を探し求めていた際、白髪の老人に化身した龍田大明神に逢い、「斑鳩の里こそが仏法興隆の地である。私はその守護神となろう」と言われたので、その地に法隆寺を建立し、鎮守社として龍田大明神を祀る神社を創建したという。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[大和国]][[平群郡]]に「龍田比古龍田比女神社二座」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列している{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 [[明治維新]]後、[[神仏分離]]によって法隆寺から分離し、境内にあった寺院関連建物は全て破却された{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）には三郷町立野の龍田神社（現在の[[龍田大社]]）の[[摂社]]となった{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。[[大正]]11年（[[1922年]]）3月に龍田大社から独立し、[[近代社格制度]]において[[県社]]に列している{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 == 境内 == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|境内社 File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|ソテツ 西株（奈良県指定天然記念物） File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|ソテツ 東株（奈良県指定天然記念物） File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|クス File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|[[金剛流]]発祥之地碑 == 文化財 == === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 天然記念物 ** ソテツの巨樹 - 昭和32年（1957年）6月13日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf 奈良県指定文化財一覧]}}（奈良県ホームページ）。。 === 関連文化財 === * [[法界寺]]本堂（薬師堂） - 国の重要文化財（建造物）。龍田神社神宮寺の伝燈寺（現在は廃寺）から法界寺（[[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]]）へ移築されたもの。明治42年（1909年）4月5日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|1917|法界寺本堂（薬師堂）}}。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[斑鳩町]]龍田1-5-6 '''交通アクセス''' * [[西日本旅客鉄道]]（JR西日本）[[大和路線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道]]（近鉄）[[王寺駅]]から、[[奈良交通]]バスで「龍田神社前」下車（乗車時間約8分、下車後徒歩すぐ） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=龍田神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[龍田大社]] * [[法隆寺]] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たつたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:斑鳩町の建築物]] [[Category:斑鳩町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]]

[[国御柱命|国御柱大神]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[県社]] |創建= |例祭=[[10月15日]]に近い日曜日 |本殿=[[流造]] |画像=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''龍田神社'''（たつたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[斑鳩町]]龍田にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == 現在の祭神は次の通り境内説明板。。 ; 本殿 :* '''[[天御柱命|天御柱大神]]''' :* '''[[国御柱命|国御柱大神]]''' ; 右社殿 :* 龍田比古大神 :* 龍田比女大神 ; 左社殿 :* [[アメノコヤネ|天児屋根大神]] :* 外三大神 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]における祭神の記載は2座。同帳では「龍田比古龍田比女神社二座」と記載され、元々の祭神は龍田比古神・龍田比女神の2柱であることが知られる。これに関して、[[龍田大社]]から天御柱命・国御柱命の2神が勧請され、元々の祭神は忘れられたとする説等がある{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。『[[大和志]]』では、[[龍田大社]]（[[三郷町]]立野）の本宮に対して当社を「龍田新宮」とする{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 == 歴史 == 社伝では、[[聖徳太子]]が[[法隆寺]]の寺地を探し求めていた際、白髪の老人に化身した龍田大明神に逢い、「斑鳩の里こそが仏法興隆の地である。私はその守護神となろう」と言われたので、その地に法隆寺を建立し、鎮守社として龍田大明神を祀る神社を創建したという。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[大和国]][[平群郡]]に「龍田比古龍田比女神社二社（二座）」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列している{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 [[明治維新]]後、[[神仏分離]]によって法隆寺から分離し、境内にあった寺院関連建物は全て破却された{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）には三郷町立野の龍田神社（現在の[[龍田大社]]）の[[摂社]]となった{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。[[大正]]11年（[[1922年]]）3月に龍田大社から独立し、[[近代社格制度]]において[[県社]]に列している{{Sfn|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 == 境内 == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|境内社 File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|ソテツ 西株（奈良県指定天然記念物） File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|ソテツ 東株（奈良県指定天然記念物） File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|クス File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|[[金剛流]]発祥之地碑 == 文化財 == === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 天然記念物 ** ソテツの巨樹 - 昭和32年（1957年）6月13日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf 奈良県指定文化財一覧]}}（奈良県ホームページ）。。 === 関連文化財 === * [[法界寺]]本堂（薬師堂） - 国の重要文化財（建造物）。龍田神社神宮寺の伝燈寺（現在は廃寺）から法界寺（[[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]]）へ移築されたもの。明治42年（1909年）4月5日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|1917|法界寺本堂（薬師堂）}}。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[斑鳩町]]龍田1-5-6 '''交通アクセス''' * [[西日本旅客鉄道]]（JR西日本）[[大和路線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道]]（近鉄）[[王寺駅]]から、[[奈良交通]]バスで「龍田神社前」下車（乗車時間約8分、下車後徒歩すぐ） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=龍田神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|龍田神社（平凡社）|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=吉川正倫|year=1982|chapter=龍田比古龍田比女神社二座|title=式内社調査報告 第2巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|平群坐紀氏神社（式内社）|1982}}}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[龍田大社]] * [[法隆寺]] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たつたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:斑鳩町の建築物]] [[Category:斑鳩町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Shrine |Name=Tatsuta Shrine |Address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |Deities={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri |Image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ** Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ** Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:龍田神社]]

{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri |Image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ** Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:龍田神社]]

The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]]龍田神社]]

Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information {{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Shrine |Name=Tatsuta Shrine |Address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |Deities={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri |Image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were revision.comment: Adding [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[category:kk]][[categoy:original pages]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Shrine |Name=Tatsuta Shrine |Address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |Deities={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri |Image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the {{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Shrine |Name=Tatsuta Shrine |Address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |Deities={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri |Image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[ja [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] :[[ [[Category:new pages]] ja:[[ [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] ja: [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:test if the jawiki is actually present]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[category:kk]] [[Category:short pages that need to be lengthened in order to effectively manage the shorrt pages list in orrderr to figure out which pages to edit]] [[Category:indexfixing]] [[Category:Category]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[ja [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] :[[ ja:[[ [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] ja: [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[category:kk]] [[Category:short pages that need to be lengthened in order to effectively manage the shorrt pages list in orrderr to figure out which pages to edit]] [[Category:indexfixing]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[ja [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] :[[ ja:[[ [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] ja: [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[category:kk]] [[Category:short pages that need to be lengthened in order to effectively manage the shorrt pages list in orrderr to figure out which pages to edit]] [[Category:indexfixing]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Ryūta Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

{{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Shrine |Name=Tatsuta Shrine |Address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |Deities={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri |Image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}} '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[ja [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] :[[ ja:[[ [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] ja: [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[category:kk]] [[Category:short pages that need to be lengthened in order to effectively manage the shorrt pages list in orrderr to figure out which pages to edit]] [[Category:indexfixing]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Ryūta Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[ja:龍田神社]]
