Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}} '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[ja [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] :[[ ja:[[ [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] ja: [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[category:kk]] [[Category:short pages that need to be lengthened in order to effectively manage the shorrt pages list in orrderr to figure out which pages to edit]] [[Category:indexfixing]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Ryūta Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[ja [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] :[[ ja:[[ [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] ja: [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:kk]] [[Category:short pages that need to be lengthened in order to effectively manage the shorrt pages list in orrderr to figure out which pages to edit]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Ryūta Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

{{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Shrine |Name=Tatsuta Shrine |Address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |Deities={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}} '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[ja [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] :[[ ja:[[ The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. {{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}} '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[ja [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] :[[ ja:[[ [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] ja: [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:kk]] completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information {{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Shrine |name=Tatsuta Shrine |Address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |Deities={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}} '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[ja [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] :[[ ja:[[ revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Shrine |name=Tatsuta Shrine |Address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |Deities={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}} '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[ja [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] :[[ ja:[[ {{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Shrine |name=Tatsuta Shrine |Address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |Deities={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}} '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[ja [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] :[[ ja:[[ Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}} '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[ja [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] :[[ ja:[[ Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}} '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[ja [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] :[[ ja:[[ Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}} '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[ja [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] :[[ ja:[[ Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}} '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[ja [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] :[[ ja:[[ Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}} '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[ja [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] :[[ ja:[[ Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}} '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[ja [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] :[[ ja:[[ This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (approx. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and {{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |Address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (aqqrox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]]
