revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |Address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (aqqrox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:龍田神社]] revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |Address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] ja: [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[category:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |Address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] ja: [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[category:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}}== See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] Shrine]][[Category:11]][[category:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ryūta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:New]] It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|12=draft}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区|12=draft}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅|12=draft}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通|12=draft}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}} ja: {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

{{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|12=draft}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|12=draft}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|12=draft}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|12=draft}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|12=draft}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|12=draft}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|12=draft}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区|12=draft}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅|12=draft}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通|12=draft}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}} ja: {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

{{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|12=draft}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}, and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|12=draft}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|12=draft}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|12=draft}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|12=draft}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|12=draft}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|12=draft}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区|12=draft}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅|12=draft}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通|12=draft}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}} ja: {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|12=draft|12=draft}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft|12=draft}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft|12=draft}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft|12=draft}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|12=draft|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|12=draft|12=draft}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通|12=draft|12=draft}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ja: {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

{{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|12=draft|12=draft}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|12=draft|12=draft}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|12=draft|12=draft}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft|12=draft}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|12=draft|12=draft}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|12=draft|12=draft}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft|12=draft}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft|12=draft}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft|12=draft}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|12=draft|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|12=draft|12=draft}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通|12=draft|12=draft}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ja: {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft|12=draft}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|12=draft|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|12=draft|12=draft}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通|12=draft|12=draft}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ja: {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Engishiki Jinmyocho|" to "Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|"
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|12=draft|12=draft}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|12=draft|12=draft}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|12=draft|12=draft}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft|12=draft}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|12=draft|12=draft}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|12=draft|12=draft}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft|12=draft}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft|12=draft}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft|12=draft}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|12=draft|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|12=draft|12=draft}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通|12=draft|12=draft}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ja: {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ja: {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]] {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a National Important Cultural Property.
