Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ikaruga, Nara]] [[Category:Ryūta Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} is a National Important Cultural Property. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōtoku|qq=}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryū-ji|qq=}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangō, Nara|qq=}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|qq=}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaruga, Nara|qq=}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji Station|qq=}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kōtsū|qq=}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryū-ji|qq=}} ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |address={{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaruga, Nara|qq=}}, Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaruga, Nara|qq=}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Taisha|qq=}}. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōtoku|qq=}} was searching for a site to establish {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryū-ji|qq=}}, he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}}'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangō, Nara|qq=}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|qq=}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}} (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in {{ill|Sangō, Nara|ja|三郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangō, Nara|qq=}}{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|qq=}} in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaruga, Nara|qq=}}, [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Ōji Station|ja|王寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji Station|qq=}} (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{ill|Nara Kōtsū|ja|奈良交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kōtsū|qq=}} bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * {{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|ja|龍田大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryū-ji|qq=}} ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |address=[[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]]. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] was searching for a site to establish [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]], he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]]'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in [[:en:Sangō, Nara|Sangō, Nara]]{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kōtsū]] bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] * [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]]

Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]]. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] was searching for a site to establish [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]], he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]]'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in [[:en:Sangō, Nara|Sangō, Nara]]{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kōtsū]] bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] * [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]]

In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in [[:en:Sangō, Nara|Sangō, Nara]]{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kōtsū]] bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] * [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:斑鳩町の建築物]] [[Category:斑鳩町の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |address=[[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]]. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] was searching for a site to establish [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]], he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]]'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in [[:en:Sangō, Nara|Sangō, Nara]]{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kōtsū]] bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] * [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:斑鳩町の建築物]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga, Nara]]

Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]]. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] was searching for a site to establish [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]], he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]]'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in [[:en:Sangō, Nara|Sangō, Nara]]{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kōtsū]] bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] * [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:斑鳩町の建築物]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga, Nara]] {{Commons category|Tatsuta-Jinja}}

It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]]. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] was searching for a site to establish [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]], he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]]'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in [[:en:Sangō, Nara|Sangō, Nara]]{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kōtsū]] bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] * [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:斑鳩町の建築物]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga, Nara]] {{Commons category|Tatsuta-Jinja}}

The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] was searching for a site to establish [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]], he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]]'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in [[:en:Sangō, Nara|Sangō, Nara]]{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kōtsū]] bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] * [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:斑鳩町の建築物]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga, Nara]] {{Commons category|Tatsuta-Jinja}}

{{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |address=[[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]]. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] was searching for a site to establish [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]], he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]]'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in [[:en:Sangō, Nara|Sangō, Nara]]{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kōtsū]] bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] * [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Ikaruga Town]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga, Nara]] {{Commons category|Tatsuta-Jinja}}

In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in [[:en:Sangō, Nara|Sangō, Nara]]{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kōtsū]] bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] * [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Ikaruga Town]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga, Nara]] {{Commons category|Tatsuta-Jinja}}
