revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11679316]] Wikidata name: Tatsuta Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |address=[[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Main Worship Hall |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]]. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] was searching for a site to establish [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]], he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]]'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in [[:en:Sangō, Nara|Sangō, Nara]]{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kōtsū]] bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] * [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Ikaruga Town]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga, Nara]] {{Commons category|Tatsuta-Jinja}}

It is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]]. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] was searching for a site to establish [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]], he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]]'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in [[:en:Sangō, Nara|Sangō, Nara]]{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kōtsū]] bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] * [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Ikaruga Town]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga, Nara]] {{Commons category|Tatsuta-Jinja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11679316]] Wikidata name: Tatsuta Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |address=[[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Shinatsuhiko|qid=Q1073925}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara |prefecture={{ill|Ikaruga|qid=Q379233}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.60833|N|135.725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]]. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] was searching for a site to establish [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]], he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]]'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in [[:en:Sangō, Nara|Sangō, Nara]]{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=|1=Hōkai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555272}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=|1=Hōkai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555272}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kōtsū]] bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] * [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Ikaruga Town]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga, Nara]] {{Commons category|Tatsuta-Jinja}}

Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=|1=Hōkai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555272}} is a National Important Cultural Property. on wikidata|WD=Q11555272}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11679316]] Wikidata name: Tatsuta Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |address=[[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Shinatsuhiko|qid=Q1073925}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara |prefecture={{ill|Ikaruga|qid=Q379233}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.60833|N|135.725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]]. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] was searching for a site to establish [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]], he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]]'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in [[:en:Sangō, Nara|Sangō, Nara]]{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=|1=Hōkai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555272}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=|1=Hōkai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555272}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kōtsū]] bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] * [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Ikaruga Town]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga, Nara]] {{Commons category|Tatsuta-Jinja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11679316]] Wikidata name: Tatsuta Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |address=[[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Shinatsuhiko|qid=Q1073925}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara |prefecture={{ill|Ikaruga|qid=Q379233}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.60833|N|135.725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]]. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] was searching for a site to establish [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]], he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]]'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in [[:en:Sangō, Nara|Sangō, Nara]]{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=|1=Hōkai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555272}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=|1=Hōkai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555272}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kōtsū]] bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] * [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Ikaruga Town]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga, Nara]] {{Commons category|Tatsuta-Jinja}} [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11679316]] Wikidata name: Tatsuta Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |address=[[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Shinatsuhiko|qid=Q1073925}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara |prefecture={{ill|Ikaruga|qid=Q379233}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.60833|N|135.725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]]. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] was searching for a site to establish [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]], he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]]'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in [[:en:Sangō, Nara|Sangō, Nara]]{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=|1=Hōkai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555272}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=|1=Hōkai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555272}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kōtsū]] bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] * [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Ikaruga Town]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga, Nara]] {{Commons category|Tatsuta-Jinja}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11679316]] Wikidata name: Tatsuta Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |address=[[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Shinatsuhiko|qid=Q1073925}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara |prefecture={{ill|Ikaruga|qid=Q379233}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.60833|N|135.725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]]. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] was searching for a site to establish [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]], he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]]'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in [[:en:Sangō, Nara|Sangō, Nara]]{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=|1=Hōkai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555272}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=|1=Hōkai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555272}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kōtsū]] bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] * [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Ikaruga Town]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga, Nara]] {{Commons category|Tatsuta-Jinja}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] was searching for a site to establish [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]], he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=|1=Hōkai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555272}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=|1=Hōkai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555272}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11679316]] Wikidata name: Tatsuta Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Tatsuta Shrine in Ikaruga, Nara|Tatsuta Shrine in Sangō, Nara|Tatsuta Taisha}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Tatsuta Shrine |address=[[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |ISO=JP-29 |Latitude_deg=34|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=30 |Longitude_deg=135|Longitude_min=43|Longitude_sec=30 |deity={{ill|Shinatsuhiko|qid=Q1073925}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established= |Festival=Sunday closest to [[October 15]] |Main_Hall=Nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Tatsuta-Jinja, haiden-1.jpg |Label_Position=top |Map=Japan Nara |prefecture={{ill|Ikaruga|qid=Q379233}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.60833|N|135.725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} [[File:Tatsuta-Jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Tatsuta Shrine''' (龍田神社, Tatsuta Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deitiesSignboard in the shrine grounds.: ; Main Hall :* '''{{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}''' :* '''{{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira|ja|国御柱命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}''' ; Right Sub-Hall :* Tatsuta-hiko Ōkami :* Tatsuta-hime Ōkami ; Left Sub-Hall :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* Three Additional Deities The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (927 CE) lists the shrine as dedicated to two deities: Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime. It is believed that these were the original enshrined deities, but they were later replaced by Ame-no-Mihashira and Kuni-no-Mihashira, who were transferred from [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]]. This change may have led to the original deities being forgotten{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The {{ill|Yamato-shi|ja|大和志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|lt=Yamato-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shi|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Kinai Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}} identifies Tatsuta Shrine as the "New Shrine" of Tatsuta Taisha{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, when [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] was searching for a site to establish [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]], he encountered Tatsuta Ōkami in the guise of an elderly man. The deity declared, "Ikaruga is a land of Buddhist flourishing. I will serve as its guardian deity." In gratitude, Prince Shōtoku established the shrine as a guardian shrine for Hōryū-ji. The shrine was listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927 CE) as "Tatsuta-hiko Tatsuta-hime Jinja, two deities" in [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]]'s Heijō District{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Buddhism and Shinto) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tatsuta Shrine was separated from Hōryū-ji, and all temple structures on its grounds were demolished{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In 1871, the shrine became a subsidiary shrine of Tatsuta Taisha in [[:en:Sangō, Nara|Sangō, Nara]]{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Tatsuta Shrine gained independence in 1922 and was designated a prefectural shrine under the modern Ranking system{{Sfn|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tatsuta-Jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sub-Shrine within the grounds File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-1.jpg|Sago Palm (designated Natural Monument of Nara Prefecture) File:Tatsuta-Jinja, sotetsu-2.jpg|Eastern Sago Palm File:Tatsuta-Jinja, kusu.jpg|Camphor Tree File:Tatsuta-Jinja, Kongoryu-sekihi.jpg|Monument marking the birthplace of {{ill|Kongō-ryū|ja|金剛流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ryū|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Designations === * Natural Monument ''' Giant Sago Palms - Designated on June 13, 1957{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. === Related Cultural Properties === * The former main hall (Yakushi-dō) of {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=|1=Hōkai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555272}} is a National Important Cultural Property. Originally located at Tatsuta Shrine’s temple (Dendenji), it was relocated to {{ill|Hōkai-ji|ja|法界寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-ji|qq=|1=Hōkai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555272}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] in 1909{{CulturalPropertyDB|102|1917|Hōkai-ji Main Hall (Yakushi-dō)}}. == Visitor Information == '''Address:''' * Tatsuta 1-5-6, [[:en:Ikaruga, Nara|Ikaruga, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access:''' * From [[:en:Ōji Station (Nara)|Ōji Station]] (JR West/Yamatoji Line and Kintetsu), take the [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kōtsū]] bus to "Tatsuta Shrine Mae" (a11rox. 8 minutes), then walk a short distance. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Shrine grounds information board * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tatsuta Shrine|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Volume 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Yoshikawa Masatoshi|year=1982|chapter=Tatsuta-hiko and Tatsuta-hime Shrine|title=Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 2|publisher=Kōgakkan University Publishing|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tatsuta Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1982}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta-Jinja}} * [[:en:Tatsuta Taisha|Tatsuta Taisha]] * [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] ja: {{translated page|ja|龍田神社|version=91469046|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Ryūta Shrine]] [[ja:龍田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Ikaruga Town]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga, Nara]] {{Commons category|Tatsuta-Jinja}}
